# Transcrição: 04-Set Up Separate Ad Account

- **Fonte:** 04-Set Up Separate Ad Account
- **Duração:** 01:49
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:49]

Okay, so guys, this has changed up a little bit. What you can do here is if you go to your business manager and you click on business settings here, and then you go to the left-hand side and you can see data sources. So if you click on data sources and then you click on pixels, you're able to select a pixel that you want to use. So let's use this one as an example here. And then essentially all you need to do is you need to assign assets or to add these assets to a different ad account. So given that we're using this pixel, you just click add assets, you then go down, and then you choose the ad account that you want to add it to. So let's say we want to add it to this one, click add, and ba-da-boom, ba-da-bing, this will now be added to the other ad account. So something really important to note here is that you want to share the pixel to the ad account where you're going to be running ads from. So you're not going to be using the pixel from the second ad account, you're going to be using the pixel from the first ad account, but you're going to run all of your campaigns from the first ad account. Now, the reason you want to do this is because more often than not, when an ad account gets shut down, you can still access the pixel. However, there are cases where you can't. So this is super helpful and it's a great way to protect yourself or the client in case an ad account does get shut down. So by doing this, you have access to the pixel data and you won't lose all the data that you acquired through your advertising spend. So essentially, you're going to be able to mitigate some of your losses or some of your client's potential losses in case Facebook decides to go on one of those account disabling frenzies again. So that's how you're able to essentially share a pixel from one ad account to another.
